gloss.en: used before a thing that is not included

words.example: Everyone came except Tom.

gloss.en: giving something and getting something back

words.example: They made a fair exchange of gifts.

gloss.en: in a way that shows happy excitement

words.example: The kids talked excitedly about the trip.

gloss.en: a feeling of being very happy and eager

words.example: The news caused great excitement among her friends.

gloss.en: very tired

words.example: After the long walk she felt exhausted.

gloss.en: a public show of art or other things

words.example: Have you seen the Picasso exhibition?

gloss.en: to become bigger or make something bigger

words.example: Metals expand when they are heated.

gloss.en: skill and knowledge gained by doing something

words.example: She has years of teaching experience.

gloss.en: having skill from doing something for a long time

words.example: He is an experienced teacher.

gloss.en: a scientific test to learn something new

words.example: We did an experiment in class today.

gloss.en: to travel around a place to learn about it

words.example: We were eager to explore the island.

gloss.en: a sudden violent bursting with a loud noise

words.example: The gas explosion damaged two houses.

gloss.en: no longer existing as a living kind

words.example: Dinosaurs have been extinct for millions of years.

gloss.en: the dying out of a kind of animal or plant

words.example: Many animals face extinction today.

gloss.en: more than usual or expected

words.example: I need some extra money this month.

gloss.en: the ability to see

words.example: Her eyesight is getting worse.

gloss.en: the front of the head

words.example: She has a pretty face.

gloss.en: with people meeting in person

words.example: We had a face-to-face meeting.

gloss.en: not strong or clear; hard to see or hear

words.example: I heard a faint sound in the dark.

gloss.en: to some degree but not very

words.example: The test was fairly easy.

gloss.en: loyal and able to be trusted

words.example: A dog is a faithful friend.

gloss.en: an act of falling down

words.example: I had a bad fall and broke my arm.

gloss.en: that has dropped to the ground

words.example: We cleared the fallen leaves from the path.

gloss.en: well known and easy to recognize

words.example: His face looked familiar to me.

gloss.en: complicated or made to impress people

words.example: They ate at a fancy restaurant.

gloss.en: a long distance away

words.example: She dreamed of a faraway country.

gloss.en: an act of saying goodbye

words.example: We said a sad farewell at the station.

gloss.en: the work of running a farm

words.example: He decided to take up farming.

gloss.en: land used for growing crops or raising animals

words.example: Miles of farmland surround the village.

gloss.en: at or to a greater distance

words.example: We walked farther into the forest.

gloss.en: at or to the greatest distance

words.example: He threw the ball farthest of all.

gloss.en: to interest someone very much

words.example: Space stories fascinate young children.

gloss.en: very interesting and attractive

words.example: She told us a fascinating story.

gloss.en: following a popular current style

words.example: She wore fashionable clothes.

gloss.en: to close or join two parts together

words.example: Please fasten your seat belts.

gloss.en: good and likely to help you

words.example: The weather was favorable for sailing.

gloss.en: a copy of a document sent by phone line

words.example: I sent the order by fax.

gloss.en: the bad feeling of being in danger

words.example: Her eyes showed no fear.

gloss.en: a large special meal for many people

words.example: They held a big feast after the wedding.

gloss.en: an important or typical part of something

words.example: An interesting feature of the city is the old market.

gloss.en: a barrier of wood or wire around an area

words.example: The two women chatted over the garden fence.

gloss.en: a boat that carries people across water

words.example: We took the ferry across the river.

gloss.en: happy and suitable for a celebration

words.example: The town had a festive mood at Christmas.

gloss.en: to go and bring back something or someone

words.example: Can you fetch me a glass of water?

gloss.en: in a very fast and excited way

words.example: They worked feverishly to finish on time.

gloss.en: number five in order

words.example: Today is the fifth of May.

gloss.en: the food or material put inside something

words.example: This cake has a sweet cream filling.

gloss.en: connected with money and finance

words.example: The company is in financial trouble.

gloss.en: one of the long thin parts of the hand

words.example: She ran her fingers through her hair.

gloss.en: flames, heat and light from burning

words.example: Most animals are afraid of fire.

gloss.en: on the floor above the ground floor

words.example: They rented a first-floor apartment.

gloss.en: the state of being healthy and strong

words.example: I read a magazine on health and fitness.

gloss.en: a cloth with a design that stands for a country

words.example: They raised the Italian flag.

gloss.en: smooth and level, without bumps

words.example: The houses had flat roofs.

gloss.en: how something tastes

words.example: This ice cream has a strong fruit flavor.

gloss.en: a group of birds or sheep

words.example: A flock of sheep crossed the road.

gloss.en: a lot of water covering dry land

words.example: The heavy rain caused a flood in the town.

gloss.en: a fine powder from grain used in cooking

words.example: Sift the flour into a bowl.

gloss.en: the steady movement of something in one direction

words.example: They measured the flow of the river.

gloss.en: an illness like a bad cold with fever

words.example: The whole family has the flu.

gloss.en: able to speak a language easily and well

words.example: She is fluent in three languages.

gloss.en: in a smooth, easy way when speaking

words.example: He speaks English fluently.

gloss.en: to fail a test or course

words.example: He was afraid he would flunk the exam.

gloss.en: a long thin musical instrument you blow into

words.example: She learned to play the flute.

gloss.en: ordinary people; traditional of common people

words.example: We visited an exhibition of folk art.

gloss.en: a feeling of liking someone or something

words.example: She has a fondness for old songs.

gloss.en: silly; not showing good sense

words.example: It was foolish to go out without a coat.

gloss.en: the part of the leg you stand on

words.example: My feet are aching.

gloss.en: not allowed by a rule or law

words.example: Smoking is forbidden in the building.

gloss.en: violent physical power used to do something

words.example: The police had to use force to stop the fight.

gloss.en: the part of the face above the eyes

words.example: He wiped the sweat from his forehead.

gloss.en: for all future time; always

words.example: I will love you forever.

gloss.en: correct and suitable for official occasions

words.example: He wore a formal suit to the meeting.

gloss.en: in an official or correct way

words.example: The deal was formally signed today.

gloss.en: a period of two weeks

words.example: We will be away for a fortnight.

gloss.en: a structure that shoots water into the air

words.example: Children played near the fountain in the park.

gloss.en: a border that holds a picture in place

words.example: She put the photo in a wooden frame.

gloss.en: to turn to ice from the cold

words.example: Water freezes at zero degrees.

gloss.en: a machine that keeps food very cold

words.example: Put the meat in the freezer.

gloss.en: extremely cold

words.example: It was freezing outside this morning.

gloss.en: often; many times

words.example: Buses run frequently between the city and the airport.

gloss.en: a student in the first year

words.example: He is a freshman at college.

gloss.en: the quality of being kind and friendly

words.example: I was touched by their friendliness.

gloss.en: a relationship between friends

words.example: They formed a close friendship at college.

gloss.en: a sudden feeling of fear

words.example: The loud bang gave me a fright.

gloss.en: to make someone feel afraid

words.example: Sorry, I didn't mean to frighten you.

gloss.en: feeling afraid

words.example: The frightened child began to cry.

gloss.en: making you feel afraid

words.example: It was a frightening experience.

gloss.en: thin white ice on cold surfaces

words.example: There was frost on the grass this morning.

gloss.en: kept very cold to preserve it

words.example: We bought a bag of frozen peas.

gloss.en: to cook something in hot oil

words.example: I will fry the eggs for breakfast.

gloss.en: the soft thick hair on some animals

words.example: The cat carefully licked its fur.

gloss.en: to put furniture in a room or house

words.example: They will furnish the new flat next week.

gloss.en: more; additional

words.example: Cook for a further two minutes.

gloss.en: at the greatest distance

words.example: That is the furthest point from home.

gloss.en: too much worry or excitement about something small

words.example: Don't make such a fuss over nothing.

gloss.en: happening at a time after now

words.example: We must protect the land for future generations.

gloss.en: rude boldness; cheek

words.example: He had the gall to ask for more money.

gloss.en: a prison (British spelling of jail)

words.example: He spent two years in gaol.

gloss.en: an empty space where something is missing

words.example: There is a gap in the fence.

gloss.en: a building for keeping a car

words.example: They parked the car in the garage.

gloss.en: the work of looking after a garden

words.example: My mother loves gardening.

gloss.en: a piece of clothing

words.example: This garment must be washed by hand.

gloss.en: to come together in one place

words.example: A crowd soon gathered.

gloss.en: used to show surprise or excitement

words.example: Gee, that was a great show!

gloss.en: affecting all or most people or things

words.example: The book is of general interest.

gloss.en: by most people; usually

words.example: The plan was generally considered a success.

gloss.en: all people born around the same time

words.example: The younger generation loves new technology.

gloss.en: happy to give money, time or help

words.example: She is generous with her time.

gloss.en: in a way related to genes

words.example: These plants are genetically the same.

gloss.en: the study of how features pass to offspring

words.example: She studies genetics at university.

gloss.en: calm, kind and careful

words.example: He is a kind and gentle man.

gloss.en: a polite and well-mannered man

words.example: You acted like a true gentleman.

gloss.en: related to geography or the earth's features

words.example: The book explains the geographical features of Asia.

gloss.en: the study of the earth's surface and places

words.example: Geography is my favorite subject.

gloss.en: the study of rocks and the earth

words.example: He teaches geology at the college.

gloss.en: the spirit of a dead person

words.example: Do you believe in ghosts?

gloss.en: very large

words.example: The film was shown on a giant screen.

gloss.en: having great natural ability

words.example: She is a gifted young musician.

gloss.en: a short, soft, silly laugh

words.example: She gave a nervous giggle.
